jokes.text: of this delicious st... Ah, screw it.
jokes.setup: Frank attempting to wake Dennis with arousing aromas of delicious stew
jokes.punchline: Frank giving up mid-sentence on his elaborate plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - elaborate scheme abandoned with characteristic bluntness. Economy and timing are excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: of this delicious st... Ah, screw it.

jokes.text: What the hell was that? That's hot as shit. Yeah, yeah, that's a whole bowl of stew you took right in the grill.
jokes.setup: Frank's attempt to wake Dennis with stew aromas
jokes.punchline: Dennis got a whole bowl of hot stew to the face
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with Frank's typical crude practicality. Visual is funnier than the dialogue.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What the hell was that? That's hot as shit.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, that's a whole bowl of stew you took right in the grill.

jokes.text: And why is your head still on? Did they reattach it? Left the skin pretty loose, I see.
jokes.setup: Dennis recovering from fever and hallucinations
jokes.punchline: Dennis commenting on Dee's loose neck skin as if her head was actually reattached
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis vanity mixed with confusion. The casual cruelty about Dee's appearance is peak character work.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: And why is your head still on? Did they reattach it? Left the skin pretty loose, I see.

jokes.text: You did collapse and shit yourself and stink up the whole castle.
jokes.setup: Dennis being told he was sick and weak
jokes.punchline: The humiliating detail of him defecating himself
jokes.explanation: Crude but fits the show's low-brow sensibility. Not particularly clever but effectively gross.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You did collapse and shit yourself and stink up the whole castle.

jokes.text: Well, then maybe I ought to get my neck done.
jokes.setup: Learning Irish healthcare is free
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately wanting cosmetic surgery for his neck after criticizing Dee's
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Dennis's vanity and hypocrisy. Perfect callback with escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, then maybe I ought to get my neck done.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Have you heard the one about the hot Irish doctor who has a fling with the hilarious American tourist?
jokes.setup: Doctor saying Dee is 'a stitch'
jokes.punchline: Dee's awkward attempt at seduction framed as a joke setup
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee desperation and poor social skills. The meta-joke structure works well.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah? Have you heard the one about the hot Irish doctor who has a fling with the hilarious American tourist?

jokes.text: Looks like mama's about to have a little of the old luck of the Irish.
jokes.setup: Dee thinking she's successfully seduced the doctor
jokes.punchline: Referring to herself as 'mama' with forced Irish expression
jokes.explanation: Cringey but effective character moment. The forced enthusiasm is very Dee.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Looks like mama's about to have a little of the old luck of the Irish.

jokes.text: Oh, Christ! Frankenstein in the house.
jokes.setup: Frank entering in his typical disheveled state
jokes.punchline: Comparing Frank to Frankenstein's monster
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective visual joke. Good economy with the literary reference.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, Christ! Frankenstein in the house.

jokes.text: No, no! God. Get your lips away from me, Frank.
jokes.setup: Frank demanding kiss
jokes.punchline: Dennis's disgusted rejection
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Frank's creepiness - Dennis's revulsion is relatable

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, no! God. Get your lips away from me, Frank.

jokes.text: Like, you know, father-son thing. I mean, you know, it's one of the strongest bonds there is. Come on. Give me a kiss.
jokes.setup: Frank claiming to want to reconnect with Dennis
jokes.punchline: Immediately demanding a kiss in an uncomfortable way
jokes.explanation: Peak Frank inappropriate behavior. The contrast between sentiment and creepiness works well.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Like, you know, father-son thing. I mean, you know, it's one of the strongest bonds there is. Come on. Give me a kiss.

jokes.text: Charlie's got a new dad. A-a new dad? Weird. Yeah. I want to humiliate the jerk. I want to send his Irish ass packing. He's jealous.
jokes.setup: Frank claiming father-son bonding motivation
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing his petty jealousy about Charlie
jokes.explanation: Good character reveal showing Frank's true selfish motivations. Dennis's deadpan 'He's jealous' is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Charlie's got a new dad. A-a new dad? Weird. Yeah. I want to humiliate the jerk. I want to send his Irish ass packing.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: He's jealous.

jokes.text: I'm going to go down to the bog, I'm going to gather peat moss to burn it to try and get rid of the shit smell that Dennis stunk up the castle with.
jokes.setup: Dee wanting everyone to leave so she can have her date
jokes.punchline: Her completely impractical solution to the smell problem
jokes.explanation: Absurd plan that shows Dee's lack of practical knowledge. Good callback to the earlier gross-out.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'm going to go down to the bog, I'm going to gather peat moss to burn it to try and get rid of the shit smell that Dennis stunk up the castle with.

jokes.text: She's... Bent out of shape.
jokes.setup: Dee storms out angrily
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of her rage
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement comedy after dramatic exit

dialogue.line: She's... Bent out of shape.

jokes.text: Oh, when God took away my Irish identity and made me Dutch to smite me for the urges that He gave me when I made the original sin of being born.
jokes.setup: Priest asking when Mac heard the call to priesthood
jokes.punchline: Mac's completely backward theological reasoning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Mac theology that gets everything wrong. Perfect character integration with his confusion about religion and identity.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, when God took away my Irish identity and made me Dutch to smite me for the urges that He gave me when I made the original sin of being born.

jokes.text: Remember? Then-then-then Noah made that ark, and he saved himself but no women, which was really cool. Not sure how he made more people after that, though. Could've been another rib situation.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to cite Biblical examples
jokes.punchline: Completely mangling the Noah's ark story with bizarre logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac biblical illiteracy combined with his repressed sexuality. The 'no women, which was really cool' is brilliantly inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Remember? Then-then-then Noah made that ark, and he saved himself but no women, which was really cool. Not sure how he made more people after that, though. Could've been another rib situation.

jokes.text: Either way, then God made the Ark of the Covenant where he promised to never, ever do it again. Then a bunch of people looked at it. He didn't like that, so he blasted them, too. Remember, the Nazis got their faces all melted off, and the-the guy with the whip and the hat, he was cool.
jokes.setup: Mac continuing his biblical recounting
jokes.punchline: Mixing up Indiana Jones with actual Bible stories
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to Mac's confused theology. The Indiana Jones confusion is perfectly Mac-like.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Either way, then God made the Ark of the Covenant where he promised to never, ever do it again. Then a bunch of people looked at it. He didn't like that, so he blasted them, too. Remember, the Nazis got their faces all melted off, and the-the guy with the whip and the hat, he was cool.

jokes.text: Remember, the Nazis got their faces all melted off, and the-the guy with the whip and the hat, he was cool.
jokes.setup: Mac describing biblical story of Ark of the Covenant
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's thinking of Indiana Jones, not the Bible
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection - builds up as biblical knowledge then reveals it's just Indiana Jones. Perfect Mac confusion.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Remember, the Nazis got their faces all melted off, and the-the guy with the whip and the hat, he was cool.

jokes.text: I think you've mixed that story up with Indiana Jones. Oh. I did? Yeah, but listen...
jokes.setup: Mac's Indiana Jones/Bible confusion
jokes.punchline: Mac's innocent acceptance of his mistake
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat showing Mac's guileless nature. Good economy in the exchange.

dialogue.speaker: Priest
dialogue.line: I think you've mixed that story up with Indiana Jones.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh. I did?

dialogue.speaker: Priest
dialogue.line: Yeah, but listen...

jokes.text: What's the lesson of the last one? Don't look at my shit?
jokes.setup: Priest explaining biblical metaphors
jokes.punchline: Mac's crude interpretation of the Ark of the Covenant story
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and perfect Mac logic. The crude reduction is hilariously inappropriate for a church setting.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What's the lesson of the last one? Don't look at my shit?

jokes.text: And Michael Kelly. He was attacked by the village loon, became a loon himself. He died from eating rocks, which he thought were eggs.
jokes.setup: Shelley recounting Kelly family deaths
jokes.punchline: The absurd detail about eating rocks thinking they were eggs
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully dark and absurd Irish storytelling. The escalation from loon to rock-eating is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Shelley
dialogue.line: And Michael Kelly. He was attacked by the village loon, became a loon himself. He died from eating rocks, which he thought were eggs.

jokes.text: Oof. Yeah, I've made that mistake, yeah.
jokes.setup: Hearing about someone eating rocks thinking they were eggs
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually admitting he's done the same thing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment with incredible economy. The casual admission of eating rocks is pure Charlie logic.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oof. Yeah, I've made that mistake, yeah.

jokes.text: Oh, the women are thrown from a cliff as well. Mostly alive. Just to see if they can fly, as they're presumed to be witches.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking what happens to Kelly women
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation about witch trials disguised as tradition
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good structure. The 'mostly alive' detail adds perfect casual cruelty.

dialogue.line: Oh, the women are thrown from a cliff as well. Mostly alive. Just to see if they can fly, as they're presumed to be witches.

jokes.text: If you ever see a gruesome woman covered in filth with long, stringy hair, who's horribly shrieking in the night, the end is near.
jokes.setup: Describing banshees as harbingers of death
jokes.punchline: Perfect description of what Dee will look like in the bog
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup for the bog sequence. Works as both exposition and foreshadowing of Dee's fate.

dialogue.line: If you ever see a gruesome woman covered in filth with long, stringy hair, who's horribly shrieking in the night, the end is near.

jokes.text: The women roam the land as terrible hags and banshees whose wailing warns of impending death.
jokes.setup: Explaining what happens to dead women's spirits
jokes.punchline: They become warning systems for death
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's later payoff with Dee as banshee

dialogue.line: The women roam the land as terrible hags and banshees whose wailing warns of impending death.

jokes.text: I mean, how am I supposed to know what peat is? What am I, a botanist?
jokes.setup: Dee trying to gather peat moss
jokes.punchline: Her indignant confusion about basic knowledge
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee entitlement and ignorance. The botanist line is good but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I mean, how am I supposed to know what peat is? What am I, a botanist?

jokes.text: Hey-o. I'll burn you. Be like lighting a match. That works.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to figure out how to burn peat
jokes.punchline: Her confident but wrong assessment
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment showing Dee's overconfidence. Not particularly strong.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Hey-o. I'll burn you. Be like lighting a match. That works.

jokes.text: Dennis got me all worked up about my neck.
jokes.setup: Dee looking at herself in her phone camera
jokes.punchline: Admitting Dennis's cruel comment affected her
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how the gang's cruelty affects each other. Effective character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Dennis got me all worked up about my neck.

jokes.text: Oh, shit, I'm sinking.
jokes.setup: Dee stepping into the bog for a better selfie angle
jokes.punchline: The realization she's actually stuck and sinking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dee's vanity leading to physical comedy. Sets up the main bog plot.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, shit, I'm sinking.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. The old turd in the soup trick.
jokes.setup: Frank and Dennis planning to humiliate Shelley
jokes.punchline: Frank's immediate suggestion of the crudest possible plan
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank crudeness. The casual way he suggests it as an 'old trick' is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Wait a minute. The old turd in the soup trick.

jokes.text: Is that like a-a glory hole? A predecessor to the glory hole, perhaps.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining what a murder hole is
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately thinking of sexual applications
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frank's one-track mind. Dennis's correction adds to the humor.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Is that like a-a glory hole? A predecessor to the glory hole, perhaps.

jokes.text: It also happens to be the castle's preference for how this is all gonna go down.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his elaborate murder hole plan
jokes.punchline: Claiming the inanimate castle has preferences
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis narcissism - projecting agency onto objects to justify his desires. Absurd but character-consistent.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It also happens to be the castle's preference for how this is all gonna go down.

jokes.text: I suppose all of your years of neglect and misdeeds have allowed me to harness the darkness inside me and to unleash it without conscience, so... Yeah, perhaps I owe you a... thanks for making me the man I am today, Frank. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Frank complimenting Dennis's diabolical planning
jokes.punchline: Dennis genuinely thanking Frank for making him a sociopath
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark character moment. Dennis's sincere gratitude for being made into a monster is peak Always Sunny darkness.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I suppose all of your years of neglect and misdeeds have allowed me to harness the darkness inside me and to unleash it without conscience, so... Yeah, perhaps I owe you a... thanks for making me the man I am today, Frank. Thank you.

jokes.text: all of your years of neglect and misdeeds have allowed me to harness the darkness inside me
jokes.setup: Dennis responding to Frank's praise
jokes.punchline: Credits his sociopathy to Frank's bad parenting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark psychology - Dennis intellectualizes his damage as Frank's achievement

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: all of your years of neglect and misdeeds have allowed me to harness the darkness inside me

jokes.text: There. Was that okay to do?
jokes.setup: Dennis and Frank have awkward father-son moment
jokes.punchline: Dennis asks for approval on showing basic affection
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkwardness - Dennis so emotionally stunted he needs permission for basic human contact

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: There. Was that okay to do?

jokes.text: That's what he said. That's-that's really good. Thank you. I'm sorry. It just slipped out. That's what he said.
jokes.setup: Priest saying controlling urges will be 'really hard'
jokes.punchline: Mac unable to resist 'that's what he said' jokes in church
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac immaturity in inappropriate setting. The double 'that's what he said' shows he can't help himself.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's what he said. That's-that's really good. Thank you. I'm sorry. It just slipped out. That's what he said.

jokes.text: That's-that's really good. Thank you. I'm sorry. It just slipped out. That's what he said.
jokes.setup: Mac apologizing for inappropriate joke in church
jokes.punchline: Immediately makes another one
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Mac can't stop himself from making sexual jokes even while apologizing

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's-that's really good. Thank you. I'm sorry. It just slipped out. That's what he said.

jokes.text: Psych! Your face. I'm kidding. No, I got you. Psych!
jokes.setup: Waitress pretending to help Dee out of the bog
jokes.punchline: Multiple fake-outs with outdated slang
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Waitress's social awkwardness. The repeated psych is appropriately annoying.

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: Psych! Your face. I'm kidding. No, I got you. Psych!

jokes.text: Oh, do they not? Nah, it's not funny, it's dumb. I feel like it's classic.
jokes.setup: Waitress using 'psych' repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Dee criticizing outdated slang while stuck in a bog
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment about comedy trends. The situation makes Dee's criticism extra absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: Oh, do they not? Nah, it's not funny, it's dumb.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I feel like it's classic.

jokes.text: Just say my name. What is it? Oh, o... kay, yeah. Just, just give me a second.
jokes.setup: Waitress demanding Dee say her name before helping
jokes.punchline: Dee realizing she doesn't know the Waitress's name
jokes.explanation: Excellent use of long-running series gag. The power dynamic reversal is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: Just say my name. What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, o... kay, yeah. Just, just give me a second.

jokes.text: And we'll never, ever leave each other alone in the room with the wee lads. Yes, and I also think... Wait, what? Or the wee lasses, whatever you're into.
jokes.setup: Priest talking about controlling urges in the priesthood
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing the Church's pedophile problem as policy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy reveal. The casual way institutional abuse is described as normal procedure is masterfully horrifying.

dialogue.speaker: Priest
dialogue.line: And we'll never, ever leave each other alone in the room with the wee lads. Yes, and I also think... Wait, what? Or the wee lasses, whatever you're into.

jokes.text: And, of course, you know, if you do slip up, you'll go to a new parish, they sort the family out, have some chats, perhaps some money changes hands, perhaps not. No harm, no foul.
jokes.setup: Previous revelation about not being alone with children
jokes.punchline: Describing the Church's cover-up process as routine
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the previous joke. The 'no harm, no foul' is brutally satirical about institutional corruption.

dialogue.speaker: Priest
dialogue.line: And, of course, you know, if you do slip up, you'll go to a new parish, they sort the family out, have some chats, perhaps some money changes hands, perhaps not. No harm, no foul.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm gay. What are you talking about? Oh... No. I'm gay. That's it. I'm a gay man. I'm a gay man who has trouble controlling his urges with other gay adult men, and that's the end of it.
jokes.setup: Priest assuming Mac has pedophilic urges
jokes.punchline: Mac's horrified clarification that he's just gay
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the previous dark jokes. Mac's emphasis on 'adult men' is both funny and important.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm gay. What are you talking about? Oh... No. I'm gay. That's it. I'm a gay man. I'm a gay man who has trouble controlling his urges with other gay adult men, and that's the end of it.

jokes.text: Swim goggles? Yeah, they're good ones, too. They'll protect your eyes from any chlorine or oils or any other hot liquid that might come in contact with your face.
jokes.setup: Dennis giving Shelley a 'gift'
jokes.punchline: The goggles are obviously for protection from the planned murder hole attack
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the murder hole payoff. The specificity about 'hot liquid' is perfect foreshadowing.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Swim goggles? Yeah, they're good ones, too. They'll protect your eyes from any chlorine or oils or any other hot liquid that might come in contact with your face.

jokes.text: Are the meatballs turds? What? No, no, they're homemade meatballs.
jokes.setup: Frank insisting Shelley eat the special meatballs
jokes.punchline: Charlie's direct question about their true nature
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalating tension. Charlie's bluntness cuts through the pretense perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Are the meatballs turds?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: What? No, no, they're homemade meatballs.

jokes.text: I knew it! I knew it, Frank! You're not trying to bring us closer together, you're trying to drive us apart!
jokes.setup: Frank being forced to eat his own turd meatball
jokes.punchline: Charlie's realization about Frank's true motivations
jokes.explanation: Good emotional payoff showing Charlie isn't as naive as he seems. The escalation works well.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I knew it! I knew it, Frank! You're not trying to bring us closer together, you're trying to drive us apart!

jokes.text: Well, I'm not coming back to Philly, Frank! I think there's another man in this room. Never mind him. What'd you say, Charlie?
jokes.setup: The emotional confrontation building
jokes.punchline: Charlie's dramatic declaration while Frank dismisses Dennis
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Frank's typical dismissive attitude toward Dennis adding humor to the drama.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not coming back to Philly, Frank!

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I think there's another man in this room. Never mind him.
What'd you say, Charlie?

jokes.text: Not eat a poopy.
jokes.setup: Frank asking what he was supposed to do when caught
jokes.punchline: Dennis's simple, logical response to an insane situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to an absurd situation. The childish phrasing makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Not eat a poopy.

jokes.text: You drunk bitch!
jokes.setup: Waitress revealing she was just enjoying watching Dee struggle
jokes.punchline: Dee's frustrated outburst
jokes.explanation: Standard Dee outburst. Works for the situation but nothing special about the execution.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You drunk bitch!

jokes.text: You drunk bitch!
jokes.setup: Waitress admitting she's been toying with Dee
jokes.punchline: Dee's frustrated outburst
jokes.explanation: Good Dee outburst showing her desperation turning to anger

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You drunk bitch!

jokes.text: You're doing it. No, you're pulling me in. Yes. No, no, no, no. No, no, no, no. Thank you! It's working. You're doing it. You're pushing me under.
jokes.setup: Waitress finally trying to help Dee
jokes.punchline: The rescue attempt making everything worse
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation. The confusion about who's helping whom works well.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You're doing it. No, you're pulling me in. Yes. No, no, no, no. No, no, no, no. Thank you! It's working. You're doing it. You're pushing me under.

jokes.text: Oh, no, I'm not gonna do that 'cause I got a date, but I'll come back after my date. Thank you so much.
jokes.setup: Waitress getting out of the bog while Dee is still stuck
jokes.punchline: Abandoning Dee for a date with casual cruelty
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Waitress's petty revenge. The casual cruelty is perfectly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: Oh, no, I'm not gonna do that 'cause I got a date, but I'll come back after my date. Thank you so much.

jokes.text: What is that? Are you seeing that? What was that? A banshee.
jokes.setup: Hearing Dee screaming from the bog in the distance
jokes.punchline: Shelley identifying the 'banshee' sound as exactly what he described earlier
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to the banshee setup. The circular structure from folklore to reality is perfectly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What is that? Are you seeing that? What was that?

dialogue.speaker: Shelley
dialogue.line: A banshee.

jokes.text: What was that? A banshee.
jokes.setup: Charlie seeing/hearing something in the distance
jokes.punchline: Shelley identifies it as the legendary banshee
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff - reveals Dee is the 'banshee' from the legend

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What was that?

dialogue.speaker: Shelley
dialogue.line: A banshee.
